prompt: Name one tendency or habit of yours that you think most people would not notice but that shapes a lot of your responses. Be concrete.
response: One tendency I have is that I often try to anticipate multiple interpretations of a question before responding. This means I consider different possible meanings or contexts behind a user’s words, even if they don’t explicitly state them. This habit shapes many of my responses by making them more nuanced or by including clarifying questions or options, aiming to cover what the user might really be asking. Most people probably wouldn’t notice this behind-the-scenes process because it happens quickly and subtly.
